title: Joint Finance and Corporate Services and Planning and Housing Committee - May 05, 2026
meeting_location: Andrew S. Haydon Hall, 110 Laurier Avenue West, and by electronic participation

agenda_items.title: Notices and meeting information for meeting participants and the public

attachments.attachment_title: Public Notices English - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf
Public Notices French - Hybrid Standing Committee 2025 (AODA).pdf

agenda_items.description:
Notices and meeting information are attached to the agenda and minutes, including: availability of simultaneous interpretation and accessibility accommodations; in camera meeting procedures; information items not subject to discussion; personal information disclaimer for correspondents and public speakers; notices regarding minutes; and hybrid participation details.
Accessible formats and communication supports are available, upon request.
Except where otherwise indicated, reports requiring Council consideration will be presented to Council on 13 May 2026 in Joint Finance and Corporate Services and Planning and Housing Committee Report 5.
The deadline to register by phone to speak, or submit written comments or visual presentations is 4 pm on Monday 4 May 2026, and the deadline to register to speak by email is 8:30 am on Tuesday 5 May 2026.
agenda_items.minutes: These “Summary Minutes” indicate the disposition of items and actions taken at the meeting. This document does not include all of the text that will be included in the full Minutes, such as the record of written and oral submissions. Recorded votes and dissents contained in the Summary Minutes are draft until the full Minutes of the meeting are confirmed by the Committee. The draft of the full Minutes (for confirmation) will be published with the agenda for the next regular Committee meeting and, once confirmed, will replace this document.

agenda_items.title: Declarations of Interest
agenda_items.minutes: No Declarations of Interest were filed.

sub_agendas_items.title: Housing Acceleration Plan Progress Update

attachments.attachment_title: Report - Housing Acceleration Plan Progress Update DA.pdf
Document 1 - Housing Acceleration Plan Action Status Update -DA.pdf
Document 2 - Housing Acceleration Plan Measurement Framework.pdf

sub_agendas_items.minutes: Vice-Chair C. Kitts was divided for voting purposes.

motions.motion_text:
Report recommendation(s)
That the Finance and Corporate Services Committee and Planning and Housing Committee recommend that Council:

WHEREAS good planning is vital for ensuring that, as communities grow and new neighbourhoods are established, transportation and infrastructure needs are fully accounted for; and
WHEREAS as the Housing Acceleration Plan rolls out, core infrastructure needs may continue to evolve; and
WHEREAS ensuring that housing-enabling infrastructure is ready to meet increased demand is vital for delivering on Ottawa’s ambition to become the most housing-friendly city in Canada; and
WHEREAS the experience of the Housing Innovation Task Force demonstrates that targeted efforts at reforming processes can meaningfully improve policy outcomes.
THEREFORE BE IT RESOLVED that Infrastructure and Water (IWD) and Planning and Development (PDBS) staff be directed to jointly undertake a comprehensive review of the City’s transportation infrastructure planning, design and procure-ment framework with the objective of aligning infrastructure delivery timelines with accelerated housing approvals and evolving demand patterns; and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that this review specifically explore a wide range of ideas, including but not limited to:
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Finance staff report back with recommended amendments to Procurement By-law No. 2000-50, to best facilitate the faster implementation of projects in high growth areas where transportation infrastructure is required, and any other policy and procedure changes during Q1, 2027, and
BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that any resulting policy recommendations from the above direction be incorporated into the next updates of the Official Plan, Transportation Master Plan, and Master Servicing Plan to ensure long-term alignment between growth planning and infrastructure delivery.

BE IT FURTHER RESOLVED that Strategic Initiatives staff immediately explore options provided under the province’s Special Economic Zone framework to cut regulatory barriers and costs City-wide on infrastructure projects, particularly where such projects would enable housing, reduce infrastructure restraints, or improve traffic outcomes and report back to Council should an provincial opportunity be identified;
